revision.comment: Reverted edit by [[Special:Contribs/188.236.106.209|188.236.106.209]] ([[User talk:188.236.106.209|talk]]) to last version by LucasBrown
revision.text: {{Wikipedia how to|H:PT|WP:PIPETRICK}} {{Linking and page manipulation|linking and diffs}} The '''pipe trick''' uses the [[pipe character]] ("{{code|{{!}}}}") to save typing the label of a [[Help:piped link|piped link]] for several kinds of [[wikilink|wiki links]]. This can avoid potentially making an error while typing the label. When the last character of a link is the pipe character, the pipe trick will automatically generate the text that displays for that link. Its processing removes the parenthesized part of the link title, handles commas in the title, and removes [[WP:Namespace|namespace]] prefixes. The described processing happens at the point of saving the page data, and the generated text is saved with the rest of the page's [[wiki markup]] – thus, it is merely an aid to editing. An even better way to save keystrokes that does not need any additional characters is by simply attaching text to the link, as in "[[train]]s". The pipe trick does not work in edit summaries, within tags, or with links to sections. See [[#Where it doesn't work|§ Where it doesn't work]] below. ==Examples== Article titles: * {{code|[[Pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} results in [[Pipe (computing)|Pipe]] * {{code|[[Phoenix, Arizona{{!}}]]}} results in [[Phoenix, Arizona|Phoenix]] Pages in other namespaces * {{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability{{!}}]]}} results in [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|Verifiability]] * {{code|[[User:Example{{!}}]]}} results in [[User:Example|Example]] * {{code|[[Template:Welcome{{!}}]]}} results in [[Template:Welcome|Welcome]]. {{code|{{tl|Welcome}}}} to produce {{tl|Welcome}} is preferred instead. * {{code|[[:Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} results in [[:Category:Wikipedia|Wikipedia]]. The colon avoids categorization of the page. {{code|[[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} should not be used. {{code|[[Category:Wikipedia{{!}} ]]}} with a space after the pipe is a [[WP:SORTKEY|category sortkey]] and not the pipe trick. The next few examples demonstrate additional features and show the expanded wiki markup text that this pipe-trick process generates, which in turn generates the results in the page display. Since Wikipedia is case-insensitive in regard to the first character of the target title, it is possible to use the pipe trick for upper- or lowercase words regardless of the target page's case. * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]] As with all links, it is possible to blend a suffix when using the pipe trick as well: * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]s}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]s}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]s * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]mill}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]mill}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]mill ==Combinations== The basic rule when the pipe trick is used is that an (initial) [[WP:namespace|namespace]] prefix, a final part beginning with a comma, or a final parenthesis (usually intended for disambiguation) is suppressed in the rendering of the link. The rendered text is case neutral and so should be entered precisely as the user wishes it to appear in the article. In case of combinations of suppressible parts, the following rules apply: ===Commas beat parentheses=== If the link name involves both one or more commas ''and'' a final parenthesis, only the parenthesis is suppressed: :{{code|[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film){{!}}]]}} is shown on the page as: :[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film)|Yours, Mine and Ours]] ===Only one prefix suppressed=== If there are several prefixes, which can occur, for example, for an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]], only the first is suppressed: :{{code|[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas{{!}}]]}} is rendered as: :[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas|Wikipedia:Políticas]] ===Only the first comma=== If there are several commas, only the part up to the first comma is shown: :{{code|[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina{{!}}]]}} results in: :[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina|Elgin]] ===Other combinations=== For the rest, if there is both an initial part and a final part that can be omitted, both are suppressed: :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian){{!}}]]}} produces: :[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian)|Manual of Style]] ==Where it doesn't work== Where the pipe trick doesn't work, the link must be written out in full manually. For example, if [[Roger Taylor (author)|]] doesn't work then write the full [[Roger Taylor (author)|Roger Taylor]]. ===<ref> footnotes and <gallery> tags=== {{tracked|T4700}} The trick does not work when enclosing between [[wikipedia:footnotes|"ref" tags]] or [[WP:Gallery|gallery tags]]. :[[Roger Taylor (author)|]][[Roger Taylor (author)|]] renders in the references list (generated by the tag) as: Note: This does not apply to references defined via {{tl|r}} or {{tl|refn}}. ===Edit summaries=== The trick does not work in edit summaries. :{{code|/* Edit summaries and the pipe trick */ as tested in [[WP:SAND{{!}}]]}} renders in the edit history as: :[[#Edit summaries and the pipe trick|→‎Edit summaries and the pipe trick]]: as tested in [[WP:SAND]] ===Section links=== The pipe trick does not work on [[Help:Link#Section linking (anchors)|section links]], and in general it does not work for links to anchors (links that use a {{code|#}} sign). For example, :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources{{!}}]]}} renders as: [[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources|]] It does not give you any wikilink. See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Wikipedia link help]]

An even better way to save keystrokes that does not need any additional characters is by simply attaching text to the link, as in "[[train]]s". The pipe trick does not work in edit summaries, within tags, or with links to sections. See [[#Where it doesn't work|§ Where it doesn't work]] below. ==Examples== Article titles: * {{code|[[Pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} results in [[Pipe (computing)|Pipe]] * {{code|[[Phoenix, Arizona{{!}}]]}} results in [[Phoenix, Arizona|Phoenix]] Pages in other namespaces * {{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability{{!}}]]}} results in [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|Verifiability]] * {{code|[[User:Example{{!}}]]}} results in [[User:Example|Example]] * {{code|[[Template:Welcome{{!}}]]}} results in [[Template:Welcome|Welcome]]. {{code|{{tl|Welcome}}}} to produce {{tl|Welcome}} is preferred instead. * {{code|[[:Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} results in [[:Category:Wikipedia|Wikipedia]]. The colon avoids categorization of the page. {{code|[[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} should not be used. {{code|[[Category:Wikipedia{{!}} ]]}} with a space after the pipe is a [[WP:SORTKEY|category sortkey]] and not the pipe trick. The next few examples demonstrate additional features and show the expanded wiki markup text that this pipe-trick process generates, which in turn generates the results in the page display. Since Wikipedia is case-insensitive in regard to the first character of the target title, it is possible to use the pipe trick for upper- or lowercase words regardless of the target page's case. * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]] As with all links, it is possible to blend a suffix when using the pipe trick as well: * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]s}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]s}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]s * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]mill}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]mill}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]mill ==Combinations== The basic rule when the pipe trick is used is that an (initial) [[WP:namespace|namespace]] prefix, a final part beginning with a comma, or a final parenthesis (usually intended for disambiguation) is suppressed in the rendering of the link. The rendered text is case neutral and so should be entered precisely as the user wishes it to appear in the article. In case of combinations of suppressible parts, the following rules apply: ===Commas beat parentheses=== If the link name involves both one or more commas ''and'' a final parenthesis, only the parenthesis is suppressed: :{{code|[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film){{!}}]]}} is shown on the page as: :[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film)|Yours, Mine and Ours]] ===Only one prefix suppressed=== If there are several prefixes, which can occur, for example, for an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]], only the first is suppressed: :{{code|[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas{{!}}]]}} is rendered as: :[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas|Wikipedia:Políticas]] ===Only the first comma=== If there are several commas, only the part up to the first comma is shown: :{{code|[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina{{!}}]]}} results in: :[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina|Elgin]] ===Other combinations=== For the rest, if there is both an initial part and a final part that can be omitted, both are suppressed: :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian){{!}}]]}} produces: :[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian)|Manual of Style]] ==Where it doesn't work== Where the pipe trick doesn't work, the link must be written out in full manually. For example, if [[Roger Taylor (author)|]] doesn't work then write the full [[Roger Taylor (author)|Roger Taylor]]. ===<ref> footnotes and <gallery> tags=== {{tracked|T4700}} The trick does not work when enclosing between [[wikipedia:footnotes|"ref" tags]] or [[WP:Gallery|gallery tags]]. :[[Roger Taylor (author)|]][[Roger Taylor (author)|]] renders in the references list (generated by the tag) as: Note: This does not apply to references defined via {{tl|r}} or {{tl|refn}}. ===Edit summaries=== The trick does not work in edit summaries. :{{code|/* Edit summaries and the pipe trick */ as tested in [[WP:SAND{{!}}]]}} renders in the edit history as: :[[#Edit summaries and the pipe trick|→‎Edit summaries and the pipe trick]]: as tested in [[WP:SAND]] ===Section links=== The pipe trick does not work on [[Help:Link#Section linking (anchors)|section links]], and in general it does not work for links to anchors (links that use a {{code|#}} sign). For example, :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources{{!}}]]}} renders as: [[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources|]] It does not give you any wikilink. See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Wikipedia link help]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia07]] [[en:Pipe trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia07]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia07]] to [[Category:dd]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {{Wikipedia how to|H:PT|WP:PIPETRICK}} {{Linking and page manipulation|linking and diffs}} The '''pipe trick''' uses the [[pipe character]] ("{{code|{{!}}}}") to save typing the label of a [[Help:piped link|piped link]] for several kinds of [[wikilink|wiki links]]. This can avoid potentially making an error while typing the label. When the last character of a link is the pipe character, the pipe trick will automatically generate the text that displays for that link. Its processing removes the parenthesized part of the link title, handles commas in the title, and removes [[WP:Namespace|namespace]] prefixes. The described processing happens at the point of saving the page data, and the generated text is saved with the rest of the page's [[wiki markup]] – thus, it is merely an aid to editing. An even better way to save keystrokes that does not need any additional characters is by simply attaching text to the link, as in "[[train]]s". The pipe trick does not work in edit summaries, within tags, or with links to sections. See [[#Where it doesn't work|§ Where it doesn't work]] below. ==Examples== Article titles: * {{code|[[Pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} results in [[Pipe (computing)|Pipe]] * {{code|[[Phoenix, Arizona{{!}}]]}} results in [[Phoenix, Arizona|Phoenix]] Pages in other namespaces * {{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability{{!}}]]}} results in [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|Verifiability]] * {{code|[[User:Example{{!}}]]}} results in [[User:Example|Example]] * {{code|[[Template:Welcome{{!}}]]}} results in [[Template:Welcome|Welcome]]. {{code|{{tl|Welcome}}}} to produce {{tl|Welcome}} is preferred instead. * {{code|[[:Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} results in [[:Category:Wikipedia|Wikipedia]]. The colon avoids categorization of the page. {{code|[[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} should not be used. {{code|[[Category:Wikipedia{{!}} ]]}} with a space after the pipe is a [[WP:SORTKEY|category sortkey]] and not the pipe trick. Since Wikipedia is case-insensitive in regard to the first character of the target title, it is possible to use the pipe trick for upper- or lowercase words regardless of the target page's case. * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]] As with all links, it is possible to blend a suffix when using the pipe trick as well: * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]s}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]s}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]s * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]mill}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]mill}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]mill ==Combinations== The basic rule when the pipe trick is used is that an (initial) [[WP:namespace|namespace]] prefix, a final part beginning with a comma, or a final parenthesis (usually intended for disambiguation) is suppressed in the rendering of the link. The rendered text is case neutral and so should be entered precisely as the user wishes it to appear in the article. In case of combinations of suppressible parts, the following rules apply: ===Commas beat parentheses=== If the link name involves both one or more commas ''and'' a final parenthesis, only the parenthesis is suppressed: :{{code|[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film){{!}}]]}} is shown on the page as: :[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film)|Yours, Mine and Ours]] ===Only one prefix suppressed=== If there are several prefixes, which can occur, for example, for an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]], only the first is suppressed: :{{code|[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas{{!}}]]}} is rendered as: :[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas|Wikipedia:Políticas]] ===Only the first comma=== If there are several commas, only the part up to the first comma is shown: :{{code|[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina{{!}}]]}} results in: :[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina|Elgin]] ===Other combinations=== For the rest, if there is both an initial part and a final part that can be omitted, both are suppressed: :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian){{!}}]]}} produces: :[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian)|Manual of Style]] ==Where it doesn't work== Where the pipe trick doesn't work, the link must be written out in full manually. For example, if [[Roger Taylor (author)|]] doesn't work then write the full [[Roger Taylor (author)|Roger Taylor]]. ===<ref> footnotes and <gallery> tags=== {{tracked|T4700}} The trick does not work when enclosing between [[wikipedia:footnotes|"ref" tags]] or [[WP:Gallery|gallery tags]]. :[[Roger Taylor (author)|]][[Roger Taylor (author)|]] renders in the references list (generated by the tag) as: Note: This does not apply to references defined via {{tl|r}} or {{tl|refn}}. ===Edit summaries=== The trick does not work in edit summaries. :{{code|/* Edit summaries and the pipe trick */ as tested in [[WP:SAND{{!}}]]}} renders in the edit history as: :[[#Edit summaries and the pipe trick|→‎Edit summaries and the pipe trick]]: as tested in [[WP:SAND]] ===Section links=== The pipe trick does not work on [[Help:Link#Section linking (anchors)|section links]], and in general it does not work for links to anchors (links that use a {{code|#}} sign). For example, :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources{{!}}]]}} renders as: [[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources|]] It does not give you any wikilink. See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] doing this to split off the two wikipedia derived categories using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

Its processing removes the parenthesized part of the link title, handles commas in the title, and removes [[WP:Namespace|namespace]] prefixes. The described processing happens at the point of saving the page data, and the generated text is saved with the rest of the page's [[wiki markup]] – thus, it is merely an aid to editing. An even better way to save keystrokes that does not need any additional characters is by simply attaching text to the link, as in "[[train]]s". The pipe trick does not work in edit summaries, within tags, or with links to sections. See [[#Where it doesn't work|§ Where it doesn't work]] below. ==Examples== Article titles: * {{code|[[Pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} results in [[Pipe (computing)|Pipe]] * {{code|[[Phoenix, Arizona{{!}}]]}} results in [[Phoenix, Arizona|Phoenix]] Pages in other namespaces * {{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability{{!}}]]}} results in [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|Verifiability]] * {{code|[[User:Example{{!}}]]}} results in [[User:Example|Example]] * {{code|[[Template:Welcome{{!}}]]}} results in [[Template:Welcome|Welcome]]. {{code|{{tl|Welcome}}}} to produce {{tl|Welcome}} is preferred instead. * {{code|[[:Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} results in [[:Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia|Wikipedia]]. The colon avoids categorization of the page. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} should not be used. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}} ]]}} with a space after the pipe is a [[WP:SORTKEY|Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category sortkey]] and not the pipe trick. The next few examples demonstrate additional features and show the expanded wiki markup text that this pipe-trick process generates, which in turn generates the results in the page display. Since Wikipedia is case-insensitive in regard to the first character of the target title, it is possible to use the pipe trick for upper- or lowercase words regardless of the target page's case. * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]] As with all links, it is possible to blend a suffix when using the pipe trick as well: * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]s}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]s}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]s * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]mill}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]mill}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]mill ==Combinations== The basic rule when the pipe trick is used is that an (initial) [[WP:namespace|namespace]] prefix, a final part beginning with a comma, or a final parenthesis (usually intended for disambiguation) is suppressed in the rendering of the link. The rendered text is case neutral and so should be entered precisely as the user wishes it to appear in the article. In case of combinations of suppressible parts, the following rules apply: ===Commas beat parentheses=== If the link name involves both one or more commas ''and'' a final parenthesis, only the parenthesis is suppressed: :{{code|[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film){{!}}]]}} is shown on the page as: :[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film)|Yours, Mine and Ours]] ===Only one prefix suppressed=== If there are several prefixes, which can occur, for example, for an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]], only the first is suppressed: :{{code|[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas{{!}}]]}} is rendered as: :[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas|Wikipedia:Políticas]] ===Only the first comma=== If there are several commas, only the part up to the first comma is shown: :{{code|[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina{{!}}]]}} results in: :[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina|Elgin]] ===Other combinations=== For the rest, if there is both an initial part and a final part that can be omitted, both are suppressed: :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian){{!}}]]}} produces: :[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian)|Manual of Style]] ==Where it doesn't work== Where the pipe trick doesn't work, the link must be written out in full manually. For example, if [[Roger Taylor (author)|]] doesn't work then write the full [[Roger Taylor (author)|Roger Taylor]]. ===<ref> footnotes and <gallery> tags=== {{tracked|T4700}} The trick does not work when enclosing between [[wikipedia:footnotes|"ref" tags]] or [[WP:Gallery|gallery tags]]. :[[Roger Taylor (author)|]][[Roger Taylor (author)|]] renders in the references list (generated by the tag) as: Note: This does not apply to references defined via {{tl|r}} or {{tl|refn}}. ===Edit summaries=== The trick does not work in edit summaries. :{{code|/* Edit summaries and the pipe trick */ as tested in [[WP:SAND{{!}}]]}} renders in the edit history as: :[[#Edit summaries and the pipe trick|→‎Edit summaries and the pipe trick]]: as tested in [[WP:SAND]] ===Section links=== The pipe trick does not work on [[Help:Link#Section linking (anchors)|section links]], and in general it does not work for links to anchors (links that use a {{code|#}} sign). For example, :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources{{!}}]]}} renders as: [[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources|]] It does not give you any wikilink. See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia link help]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Pipe trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:dd]]

revision.text: {{Wikipedia how to|H:PT|WP:PIPETRICK}} {{Linking and page manipulation|linking and diffs}} The '''pipe trick''' uses the [[pipe character]] ("{{code|{{!}}}}") to save typing the label of a [[Help:piped link|piped link]] for several kinds of [[wikilink|wiki links]]. This can avoid potentially making an error while typing the label. When the last character of a link is the pipe character, the pipe trick will automatically generate the text that displays for that link. Its processing removes the parenthesized part of the link title, handles commas in the title, and removes [[WP:Namespace|namespace]] prefixes. The described processing happens at the point of saving the page data, and the generated text is saved with the rest of the page's [[wiki markup]] – thus, it is merely an aid to editing. An even better way to save keystrokes that does not need any additional characters is by simply attaching text to the link, as in "[[train]]s". The pipe trick does not work in edit summaries, within tags, or with links to sections. See [[#Where it doesn't work|§ Where it doesn't work]] below. ==Examples== Article titles: * {{code|[[Pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} results in [[Pipe (computing)|Pipe]] * {{code|[[Phoenix, Arizona{{!}}]]}} results in [[Phoenix, Arizona|Phoenix]] Pages in other namespaces * {{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability{{!}}]]}} results in [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|Verifiability]] * {{code|[[User:Example{{!}}]]}} results in [[User:Example|Example]] * {{code|[[Template:Welcome{{!}}]]}} results in [[Template:Welcome|Welcome]]. {{code|{{tl|Welcome}}}} to produce {{tl|Welcome}} is preferred instead. * {{code|[[:Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} results in [[:Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia|Wikipedia]]. The colon avoids categorization of the page. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} should not be used. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}} ]]}} with a space after the pipe is a [[WP:SORTKEY|Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category sortkey]] and not the pipe trick. The next few examples demonstrate additional features and show the expanded wiki markup text that this pipe-trick process generates, which in turn generates the results in the page display. Since Wikipedia is case-insensitive in regard to the first character of the target title, it is possible to use the pipe trick for upper- or lowercase words regardless of the target page's case. * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]] As with all links, it is possible to blend a suffix when using the pipe trick as well: * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]s}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]s}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]s * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]mill}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]mill}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]mill ==Combinations== The basic rule when the pipe trick is used is that an (initial) [[WP:namespace|namespace]] prefix, a final part beginning with a comma, or a final parenthesis (usually intended for disambiguation) is suppressed in the rendering of the link. The rendered text is case neutral and so should be entered precisely as the user wishes it to appear in the article. In case of combinations of suppressible parts, the following rules apply: ===Commas beat parentheses=== If the link name involves both one or more commas ''and'' a final parenthesis, only the parenthesis is suppressed: :{{code|[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film){{!}}]]}} is shown on the page as: :[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film)|Yours, Mine and Ours]] ===Only one prefix suppressed=== If there are several prefixes, which can occur, for example, for an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]], only the first is suppressed: :{{code|[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas{{!}}]]}} is rendered as: :[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas|Wikipedia:Políticas]] ===Only the first comma=== If there are several commas, only the part up to the first comma is shown: :{{code|[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina{{!}}]]}} results in: :[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina|Elgin]] ===Other combinations=== For the rest, if there is both an initial part and a final part that can be omitted, both are suppressed: :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian){{!}}]]}} produces: :[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian)|Manual of Style]] ==Where it doesn't work== Where the pipe trick doesn't work, the link must be written out in full manually. For example, if [[Roger Taylor (author)|]] doesn't work then write the full [[Roger Taylor (author)|Roger Taylor]]. ===<ref> footnotes and <gallery> tags=== {{tracked|T4700}} The trick does not work when enclosing between [[wikipedia:footnotes|"ref" tags]] or [[WP:Gallery|gallery tags]]. :[[Roger Taylor (author)|]][[Roger Taylor (author)|]] renders in the references list (generated by the tag) as: Note: This does not apply to references defined via {{tl|r}} or {{tl|refn}}. ===Edit summaries=== The trick does not work in edit summaries. :{{code|/* Edit summaries and the pipe trick */ as tested in [[WP:SAND{{!}}]]}} renders in the edit history as: :[[#Edit summaries and the pipe trick|→‎Edit summaries and the pipe trick]]: as tested in [[WP:SAND]] ===Section links=== The pipe trick does not work on [[Help:Link#Section linking (anchors)|section links]], and in general it does not work for links to anchors (links that use a {{code|#}} sign). For example, :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources{{!}}]]}} renders as: [[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources|]] It does not give you any wikilink. See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia link help]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Pipe trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:dd]]

See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia link help]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Pipe trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:Images]]

revision.comment: fixing error I did on all these pages
revision.text: {{Wikipedia how to|H:PT|WP:PIPETRICK}} {{Linking and page manipulation|linking and diffs}} The '''pipe trick''' uses the [[pipe character]] ("{{code|{{!}}}}") to save typing the label of a [[Help:piped link|piped link]] for several kinds of [[wikilink|wiki links]]. This can avoid potentially making an error while typing the label. When the last character of a link is the pipe character, the pipe trick will automatically generate the text that displays for that link. Its processing removes the parenthesized part of the link title, handles commas in the title, and removes [[WP:Namespace|namespace]] prefixes. The described processing happens at the point of saving the page data, and the generated text is saved with the rest of the page's [[wiki markup]] – thus, it is merely an aid to editing. An even better way to save keystrokes that does not need any additional characters is by simply attaching text to the link, as in "[[train]]s". The pipe trick does not work in edit summaries, within tags, or with links to sections. See [[#Where it doesn't work|§ Where it doesn't work]] below. ==Examples== Article titles: * {{code|[[Pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} results in [[Pipe (computing)|Pipe]] * {{code|[[Phoenix, Arizona{{!}}]]}} results in [[Phoenix, Arizona|Phoenix]] Pages in other namespaces * {{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability{{!}}]]}} results in [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|Verifiability]] * {{code|[[User:Example{{!}}]]}} results in [[User:Example|Example]] * {{code|[[Template:Welcome{{!}}]]}} results in [[Template:Welcome|Welcome]]. {{code|{{tl|Welcome}}}} to produce {{tl|Welcome}} is preferred instead. * {{code|[[:Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} results in [[:Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia|Wikipedia]]. The colon avoids categorization of the page. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} should not be used. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}} ]]}} with a space after the pipe is a [[WP:SORTKEY|Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category sortkey]] and not the pipe trick. The next few examples demonstrate additional features and show the expanded wiki markup text that this pipe-trick process generates, which in turn generates the results in the page display. Since Wikipedia is case-insensitive in regard to the first character of the target title, it is possible to use the pipe trick for upper- or lowercase words regardless of the target page's case. * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]] As with all links, it is possible to blend a suffix when using the pipe trick as well: * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]s}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]s}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]s * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]mill}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]mill}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]mill ==Combinations== The basic rule when the pipe trick is used is that an (initial) [[WP:namespace|namespace]] prefix, a final part beginning with a comma, or a final parenthesis (usually intended for disambiguation) is suppressed in the rendering of the link. The rendered text is case neutral and so should be entered precisely as the user wishes it to appear in the article. In case of combinations of suppressible parts, the following rules apply: ===Commas beat parentheses=== If the link name involves both one or more commas ''and'' a final parenthesis, only the parenthesis is suppressed: :{{code|[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film){{!}}]]}} is shown on the page as: :[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film)|Yours, Mine and Ours]] ===Only one prefix suppressed=== If there are several prefixes, which can occur, for example, for an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]], only the first is suppressed: :{{code|[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas{{!}}]]}} is rendered as: :[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas|Wikipedia:Políticas]] ===Only the first comma=== If there are several commas, only the part up to the first comma is shown: :{{code|[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina{{!}}]]}} results in: :[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina|Elgin]] ===Other combinations=== For the rest, if there is both an initial part and a final part that can be omitted, both are suppressed: :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian){{!}}]]}} produces: :[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian)|Manual of Style]] ==Where it doesn't work== Where the pipe trick doesn't work, the link must be written out in full manually. For example, if [[Roger Taylor (author)|]] doesn't work then write the full [[Roger Taylor (author)|Roger Taylor]]. ===<ref> footnotes and <gallery> tags=== {{tracked|T4700}} The trick does not work when enclosing between [[wikipedia:footnotes|"ref" tags]] or [[WP:Gallery|gallery tags]]. :[[Roger Taylor (author)|]][[Roger Taylor (author)|]] renders in the references list (generated by the tag) as: Note: This does not apply to references defined via {{tl|r}} or {{tl|refn}}. ===Edit summaries=== The trick does not work in edit summaries. :{{code|/* Edit summaries and the pipe trick */ as tested in [[WP:SAND{{!}}]]}} renders in the edit history as: :[[#Edit summaries and the pipe trick|→‎Edit summaries and the pipe trick]]: as tested in [[WP:SAND]] ===Section links=== The pipe trick does not work on [[Help:Link#Section linking (anchors)|section links]], and in general it does not work for links to anchors (links that use a {{code|#}} sign). For example, :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources{{!}}]]}} renders as: [[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources|]] It does not give you any wikilink. See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia link help]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Pipe trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:Images]]

Since Wikipedia is case-insensitive in regard to the first character of the target title, it is possible to use the pipe trick for upper- or lowercase words regardless of the target page's case. * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]] As with all links, it is possible to blend a suffix when using the pipe trick as well: * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]s}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]s}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]s * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]mill}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]mill}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]mill ==Combinations== The basic rule when the pipe trick is used is that an (initial) [[WP:namespace|namespace]] prefix, a final part beginning with a comma, or a final parenthesis (usually intended for disambiguation) is suppressed in the rendering of the link. The rendered text is case neutral and so should be entered precisely as the user wishes it to appear in the article. In case of combinations of suppressible parts, the following rules apply: ===Commas beat parentheses=== If the link name involves both one or more commas ''and'' a final parenthesis, only the parenthesis is suppressed: :{{code|[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film){{!}}]]}} is shown on the page as: :[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film)|Yours, Mine and Ours]] ===Only one prefix suppressed=== If there are several prefixes, which can occur, for example, for an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]], only the first is suppressed: :{{code|[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas{{!}}]]}} is rendered as: :[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas|Wikipedia:Políticas]] ===Only the first comma=== If there are several commas, only the part up to the first comma is shown: :{{code|[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina{{!}}]]}} results in: :[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina|Elgin]] ===Other combinations=== For the rest, if there is both an initial part and a final part that can be omitted, both are suppressed: :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian){{!}}]]}} produces: :[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian)|Manual of Style]] ==Where it doesn't work== Where the pipe trick doesn't work, the link must be written out in full manually. For example, if [[Roger Taylor (author)|]] doesn't work then write the full [[Roger Taylor (author)|Roger Taylor]]. ===<ref> footnotes and <gallery> tags=== {{tracked|T4700}} The trick does not work when enclosing between [[wikipedia:footnotes|"ref" tags]] or [[WP:Gallery|gallery tags]]. :[[Roger Taylor (author)|]][[Roger Taylor (author)|]] renders in the references list (generated by the tag) as: Note: This does not apply to references defined via {{tl|r}} or {{tl|refn}}. ===Edit summaries=== The trick does not work in edit summaries. :{{code|/* Edit summaries and the pipe trick */ as tested in [[WP:SAND{{!}}]]}} renders in the edit history as: :[[#Edit summaries and the pipe trick|→‎Edit summaries and the pipe trick]]: as tested in [[WP:SAND]] ===Section links=== The pipe trick does not work on [[Help:Link#Section linking (anchors)|section links]], and in general it does not work for links to anchors (links that use a {{code|#}} sign). For example, :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources{{!}}]]}} renders as: [[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources|]] It does not give you any wikilink. See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia link help]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Pipe trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]]

{{Wikipedia how to|H:PT|WP:PIPETRICK}} {{Linking and page manipulation|linking and diffs}} The '''pipe trick''' uses the [[pipe character]] ("{{code|{{!}}}}") to save typing the label of a [[Help:piped link|piped link]] for several kinds of [[wikilink|wiki links]]. This can avoid potentially making an error while typing the label. When the last character of a link is the pipe character, the pipe trick will automatically generate the text that displays for that link. Its processing removes the parenthesized part of the link title, handles commas in the title, and removes [[WP:Namespace|namespace]] prefixes. The described processing happens at the point of saving the page data, and the generated text is saved with the rest of the page's [[wiki markup]] – thus, it is merely an aid to editing. An even better way to save keystrokes that does not need any additional characters is by simply attaching text to the link, as in "[[train]]s". The pipe trick does not work in edit summaries, within tags, or with links to sections. See [[#Where it doesn't work|§ Where it doesn't work]] below. ==Examples== Article titles: * {{code|[[Pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} results in [[Pipe (computing)|Pipe]] * {{code|[[Phoenix, Arizona{{!}}]]}} results in [[Phoenix, Arizona|Phoenix]] Pages in other namespaces * {{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability{{!}}]]}} results in [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|Verifiability]] * {{code|[[User:Example{{!}}]]}} results in [[User:Example|Example]] * {{code|[[Template:Welcome{{!}}]]}} results in [[Template:Welcome|Welcome]]. {{code|{{tl|Welcome}}}} to produce {{tl|Welcome}} is preferred instead. * {{code|[[:Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} results in [[:Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia|Wikipedia]]. The colon avoids categorization of the page. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} should not be used. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia{{!}} ]]}} with a space after the pipe is a [[WP:SORTKEY|Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category sortkey]] and not the pipe trick. The next few examples demonstrate additional features and show the expanded wiki markup text that this pipe-trick process generates, which in turn generates the results in the page display. Since Wikipedia is case-insensitive in regard to the first character of the target title, it is possible to use the pipe trick for upper- or lowercase words regardless of the target page's case. * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]] As with all links, it is possible to blend a suffix when using the pipe trick as well: * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]s}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]s}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]s * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]mill}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]mill}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]mill ==Combinations== The basic rule when the pipe trick is used is that an (initial) [[WP:namespace|namespace]] prefix, a final part beginning with a comma, or a final parenthesis (usually intended for disambiguation) is suppressed in the rendering of the link. The rendered text is case neutral and so should be entered precisely as the user wishes it to appear in the article. In case of combinations of suppressible parts, the following rules apply: ===Commas beat parentheses=== If the link name involves both one or more commas ''and'' a final parenthesis, only the parenthesis is suppressed: :{{code|[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film){{!}}]]}} is shown on the page as: :[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film)|Yours, Mine and Ours]] ===Only one prefix suppressed=== If there are several prefixes, which can occur, for example, for an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]], only the first is suppressed: :{{code|[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas{{!}}]]}} is rendered as: :[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas|Wikipedia:Políticas]] ===Only the first comma=== If there are several commas, only the part up to the first comma is shown: :{{code|[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina{{!}}]]}} results in: :[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina|Elgin]] ===Other combinations=== For the rest, if there is both an initial part and a final part that can be omitted, both are suppressed: :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian){{!}}]]}} produces: :[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian)|Manual of Style]] ==Where it doesn't work== Where the pipe trick doesn't work, the link must be written out in full manually. For example, if [[Roger Taylor (author)|]] doesn't work then write the full [[Roger Taylor (author)|Roger Taylor]]. ===<ref> footnotes and <gallery> tags=== {{tracked|T4700}} The trick does not work when enclosing between [[wikipedia:footnotes|"ref" tags]] or [[WP:Gallery|gallery tags]]. :[[Roger Taylor (author)|]][[Roger Taylor (author)|]] renders in the references list (generated by the tag) as: Note: This does not apply to references defined via {{tl|r}} or {{tl|refn}}. ===Edit summaries=== The trick does not work in edit summaries. :{{code|/* Edit summaries and the pipe trick */ as tested in [[WP:SAND{{!}}]]}} renders in the edit history as: :[[#Edit summaries and the pipe trick|→‎Edit summaries and the pipe trick]]: as tested in [[WP:SAND]] ===Section links=== The pipe trick does not work on [[Help:Link#Section linking (anchors)|section links]], and in general it does not work for links to anchors (links that use a {{code|#}} sign). For example, :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources{{!}}]]}} renders as: [[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources|]] It does not give you any wikilink. See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia link help]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Pipe trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:1 revision imported]]

See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia link help]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Pipe trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]]

{{Wikipedia how to|H:PT|WP:PIPETRICK}} {{Linking and page manipulation|linking and diffs}} The '''pipe trick''' uses the [[pipe character]] ("{{code|{{!}}}}") to save typing the label of a [[Help:piped link|piped link]] for several kinds of [[wikilink|wiki links]]. This can avoid potentially making an error while typing the label. When the last character of a link is the pipe character, the pipe trick will automatically generate the text that displays for that link. Its processing removes the parenthesized part of the link title, handles commas in the title, and removes [[WP:Namespace|namespace]] prefixes. The described processing happens at the point of saving the page data, and the generated text is saved with the rest of the page's [[wiki markup]] – thus, it is merely an aid to editing. An even better way to save keystrokes that does not need any additional characters is by simply attaching text to the link, as in "[[train]]s". The pipe trick does not work in edit summaries, within tags, or with links to sections. See [[#Where it doesn't work|§ Where it doesn't work]] below. ==Examples== Article titles: * {{code|[[Pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} results in [[Pipe (computing)|Pipe]] * {{code|[[Phoenix, Arizona{{!}}]]}} results in [[Phoenix, Arizona|Phoenix]] Pages in other namespaces * {{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability{{!}}]]}} results in [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|Verifiability]] * {{code|[[User:Example{{!}}]]}} results in [[User:Example|Example]] * {{code|[[Template:Welcome{{!}}]]}} results in [[Template:Welcome|Welcome]]. {{code|{{tl|Welcome}}}} to produce {{tl|Welcome}} is preferred instead. * {{code|[[:Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} results in [[:Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia|Wikipedia]]. The colon avoids categorization of the page. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia{{!}}]]}} should not be used. {{code|[[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia{{!}} ]]}} with a space after the pipe is a [[WP:SORTKEY|Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category sortkey]] and not the pipe trick. The next few examples demonstrate additional features and show the expanded wiki markup text that this pipe-trick process generates, which in turn generates the results in the page display. Since Wikipedia is case-insensitive in regard to the first character of the target title, it is possible to use the pipe trick for upper- or lowercase words regardless of the target page's case. * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]] As with all links, it is possible to blend a suffix when using the pipe trick as well: * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]s}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]s}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]s * {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}]]mill}} generates the wiki markup {{code|[[pipe (computing){{!}}pipe]]mill}} rendered as [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]mill ==Combinations== The basic rule when the pipe trick is used is that an (initial) [[WP:namespace|namespace]] prefix, a final part beginning with a comma, or a final parenthesis (usually intended for disambiguation) is suppressed in the rendering of the link. The rendered text is case neutral and so should be entered precisely as the user wishes it to appear in the article. In case of combinations of suppressible parts, the following rules apply: ===Commas beat parentheses=== If the link name involves both one or more commas ''and'' a final parenthesis, only the parenthesis is suppressed: :{{code|[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film){{!}}]]}} is shown on the page as: :[[Yours, Mine and Ours (1968 film)|Yours, Mine and Ours]] ===Only one prefix suppressed=== If there are several prefixes, which can occur, for example, for an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]], only the first is suppressed: :{{code|[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas{{!}}]]}} is rendered as: :[[:es:Wikipedia:Políticas|Wikipedia:Políticas]] ===Only the first comma=== If there are several commas, only the part up to the first comma is shown: :{{code|[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina{{!}}]]}} results in: :[[Elgin, Kershaw County, South Carolina|Elgin]] ===Other combinations=== For the rest, if there is both an initial part and a final part that can be omitted, both are suppressed: :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian){{!}}]]}} produces: :[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style (Persian)|Manual of Style]] ==Where it doesn't work== Where the pipe trick doesn't work, the link must be written out in full manually. For example, if [[Roger Taylor (author)|]] doesn't work then write the full [[Roger Taylor (author)|Roger Taylor]]. ===<ref> footnotes and <gallery> tags=== {{tracked|T4700}} The trick does not work when enclosing between [[Wikipedia:footnotes|"ref" tags]] or [[WP:Gallery|gallery tags]]. :[[Roger Taylor (author)|]][[Roger Taylor (author)|]] renders in the references list (generated by the tag) as: Note: This does not apply to references defined via {{tl|r}} or {{tl|refn}}. ===Edit summaries=== The trick does not work in edit summaries. :{{code|/* Edit summaries and the pipe trick */ as tested in [[WP:SAND{{!}}]]}} renders in the edit history as: :[[#Edit summaries and the pipe trick|→‎Edit summaries and the pipe trick]]: as tested in [[WP:SAND]] ===Section links=== The pipe trick does not work on [[Help:Link#Section linking (anchors)|section links]], and in general it does not work for links to anchors (links that use a {{code|#}} sign). For example, :{{code|[[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources{{!}}]]}} renders as: [[Wikipedia:Verifiability#Sources|]] It does not give you any wikilink. See [[phabricator:T2845]], "[[#foo|]], [[/bar|]] should be equivalent to [[#foo|foo]], [[/bar|bar]]". == Related tricks == === Slash trick === You can achieve a similar effect for [[help:subpage|subpage]]s by adding a slash. : {{code|[[/Subpage/]]}} generates: : [[/Subpage/]] Unlike the pipe trick, though, the slash trick is not expanded in the saved [[wiki markup]]. This trick only works in namespaces where subpages are enabled. ===Reverse pipe trick=== If the title of the article you are editing includes a parenthesized term or a comma, placing a pipe at the ''start'' of a link will append that parenthesized text to the link target, but not display it. For example, if the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Agonizer]]}} were placed in [[Agonist (disambiguation)]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Agonizer (disambiguation)|Agonizer]]}} If the link :{{code|[[{{!}}Palo Alto]]}} were placed in [[Pacific Grove, California]], the link would be expanded to :{{code|[[Palo Alto, California|Palo Alto]]}} Placing a pipe at the start of a link to an article that has no parentheses or comma in its title will have no effect. ==Alternatives== Some templates are available as alternatives to using the trick for linking. {{tbullet|user link|Example}} (shortcut: {{tl|u}}) {{tbullet|help link|Example}} {{tbullet|tlbare|Example}} ==See also== * [[Help:Magic]] * [[Help:Piped link]] * [[m:Help:Piped link]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia features|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia link help]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Pipe trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Pipe trick]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]]
